dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes waiting for technical confirmation at key levels (support/resistance) rather than entering at the level itself. Used pivot points (s1/r1) as proxies for historically significant support/resistance levels. Entry requires price to cross above/below these levels AND show directional confirmation (close > prev_close for long, close < prev_close for short). This enforces the 'double confirmation' principle stated in the rule. Exit uses nearest support/resistance as stop loss. The 0.5% bounce_threshold is incorporated into stop placement logic. Confirmation_bars_required of 1 is reflected in the AND logic requiring current bar confirmation.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal emphasizes waiting for technical confirmation at key levels (support/resistance) rather than entering at the level itself. Used pivot points (s1/r1) as proxies for historically significant support/resistance levels. Entry requires price to cross above/below these levels AND show directional confirmation (close > prev_close for long, close < prev_close for short). This enforces the 'double confirmation' principle stated in the rule. Exit uses nearest support/resistance as stop loss. The 0.5% bounce_threshold is incorporated into stop placement logic. Confirmation_bars_required of 1 is reflected in the AND logic requiring current bar confirmation.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter a position only after technical confirmation of a key level reversal, not at the level itself, even if fundamentally attractive—patience for double confirmation reduces emotional risk and drawdown.
